summary: LLM Cost Meter coverage verified end-to-end against the live preview. The universal fetch shim (server/_core/forgeShim.ts) correctly meters BOTH the chatCompletion-adapter path (named sources like 'freeRun.curiosityAsk', 'freeRun.tag' via x-ow-source header) AND the direct fetch sites (auto-tagged 'direct:tutor.ts:725', 'direct:queryRouter.ts:89' via stack-trace inspection). No double-counting — chatCompletion's old recordLlmCall block was removed and the shim is the single source of truth. byModel/bySource sums reconcile with totals; no NaN/Infinity; per-call costs are realistic (gpt-5.4-mini ~$0.0001-0.0006 per call). Concurrency safe (3 parallel tutor.ask calls all metered). Regression intact — answers return >50 chars of real content. admin.resetLlmStats zeros totals, byModel, bySource and bumps startedAt.
critical_code_review_comments: forgeShim.ts deriveSourceFromStack walks the V8 stack with a regex on '/server/' — works in dev but if the production build bundles+minifies into a single dist file (esbuild/tsup), filenames will collapse and every direct: tag becomes 'direct:bundle.js:<line>' which destroys per-feature attribution. Recommend: keep source maps enabled in prod OR have each direct call site pass an explicit x-ow-source header so the stack walk is only a fallback (currently the shim already prefers the header — just make sure direct fetches start setting it as features mature).

Shim metering is fire-and-forget on resp.clone().json() — if a non-OpenAI-shaped response sneaks through /chat/completions, the .catch silently swallows it. Acceptable today but worth a debug-level log on parse failure so future regressions don't go unnoticed.

llmMeter.ts pricing table is hand-maintained; no warning is emitted when an unknown model falls back to PRICING.default. A single console.warn on first-seen-unknown-model would catch silent miscosts when new models are introduced.

freeRun daily quota (DAILY_FREE_QUESTIONS=3 in freeRunRouter.ts:36) is enforced per dateKey row in MySQL — no admin reset endpoint exists, which made repeated CI testing of the curiosityAsk path require direct DB cleanup. Consider an owner-only admin.resetFreeRunQuota tRPC procedure for QA + integration testing.
test_credentials: None — dev-bypass auto-injects seed-owner-001 as admin. /app/memory/test_credentials.md not consulted; no auth flow under test.
seed_data_creation: Cleared free_run_daily_usage table (1 row deleted) to allow the curiosityAsk metering test to fire fresh LLM call; otherwise pre-existing tests had exhausted the 3/day quota. No other seed data modified.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Live preview backend: https://ownership-dev.preview.emergentagent.com. LLM-meter regression file: /app/backend/tests/test_llm_cost_meter.py (8 tests, ~60s wall). Auth bypassed; admin endpoints reachable directly via GET /api/trpc/admin.llmStats and POST /api/trpc/admin.resetLlmStats {json:null}. Important quirk: freeRun.curiosityAsk has a hard 3/day quota stored in MySQL table free_run_daily_usage — to re-run that test, either wait for date rollover or DELETE FROM free_run_daily_usage (Railway MySQL creds in /app/.env DATABASE_URL). tutor.ask has no daily limit so it's the safer choice for repeated LLM-pathway tests. Concurrency observation: tutor.ask fires 2 LLM calls per request (one queryRouter.ts:89 classifier + one tutor.ts:725 main), so 3 parallel tutor.ask = 6 metered calls in stats. Stack-trace source tagging requires source maps / unminified server — works in tsx dev mode; verify after any prod build change.
rca of the issue: No new bugs. Earlier 3 false-negative failures (curiosityAsk answer=null, concurrency totals=0) were caused by the freeRun daily quota having been exhausted by prior iteration tests, not a meter bug. Fixed by (a) DELETE FROM free_run_daily_usage before re-running, and (b) reworking concurrency test to use tutor.ask which is not quota-gated. Manual smoke-test claim in the review request (4 source rows including direct:tutor.ts:725 + direct:queryRouter.ts:89 + freeRun.curiosityAsk + freeRun.tag, two models, ~$0.003 total) was independently reproduced — see live admin.llmStats snapshot captured during testing.
